zayed_mosque.title: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
zayed_mosque.location: Sheikh Rashid Bin Saeed Street, Abu Dhabi
zayed_mosque.hours: Open daily; closes for Friday morning prayers. Check official site for current details
zayed_mosque.context: One of the world's largest mosques and Abu Dhabi's most iconic landmark, featuring 82 domes, reflective pools, and the world's largest hand-knotted carpet. Modest dress is required; abayas are available on loan. Free guided tours are offered.
zayed_mosque.nudge: Plan around prayer times and dress modestly — then continue to the Wahat Al Karama memorial nearby.

louvre.title: Louvre Abu Dhabi
louvre.location: Saadiyat Cultural District, Saadiyat Island, Abu Dhabi
louvre.hours: Tuesday–Sunday, closed Mondays. Check official site for current details
louvre.fee: Paid admission; free for children under a set age. Check official site for current details
louvre.context: A universal museum created in partnership with France, housed under Jean Nouvel's famous 'rain of light' dome. It displays art and artefacts spanning civilisations, from ancient times to the contemporary era.
louvre.nudge: Pair your visit with neighbouring Saadiyat Cultural District museums for a full day of art.

qasr_al_watan.title: Qasr Al Watan
qasr_al_watan.location: Al Ras Al Akhdar, Abu Dhabi
qasr_al_watan.hours: Open daily. Check official site for current details
qasr_al_watan.fee: Paid admission with multiple ticket tiers. Check official site for current details
qasr_al_watan.context: A working presidential palace open to visitors, showcasing Arabian art, heritage, and governance. Highlights include the Great Hall, the House of Knowledge library, and a nightly sound-and-light show.
qasr_al_watan.nudge: Stay for the evening 'Palace in Motion' light show projected onto the facade.

yas_island.title: Yas Island
yas_island.hours: Varies by venue and theme park. Check official site for current details
yas_island.fee: Free to enter the island; individual attractions ticketed. Check official site for current details
yas_island.context: Abu Dhabi's leisure and entertainment hub, home to Ferrari World, Yas Waterworld, Warner Bros. World, SeaWorld, the Yas Marina Circuit, and Yas Mall. A free shuttle connects the major venues.
yas_island.nudge: Bundle theme-park tickets in advance and use the free Yas Express shuttle between parks.

saadiyat_beach.title: Saadiyat Public Beach
saadiyat_beach.hours: Open daily; lifeguard hours vary. Check official site for current details
saadiyat_beach.fee: Nominal entry / facilities fee. Check official site for current details
saadiyat_beach.context: A protected, white-sand natural beach on Saadiyat Island known for clear waters and as a nesting site for endangered hawksbill turtles. Facilities include loungers, showers, and a cafe.
saadiyat_beach.nudge: Arrive early for parking and look out for nesting hawksbill turtles in season.

corniche.title: Abu Dhabi Corniche
corniche.hours: Open 24 hours; Corniche Beach has set hours. Check official site for current details
corniche.fee: Free promenade; Corniche Beach has a small fee. Check official site for current details
corniche.context: An eight-kilometre waterfront promenade with cycling and pedestrian paths, parks, cafes, and a Blue Flag beach. A favourite spot for walking, jogging, and sunset views of the city skyline.
corniche.nudge: Rent a bike along the cycle path and finish with sunset views over the skyline.

qasr_al_hosn.title: Qasr Al Hosn
qasr_al_hosn.location: Al Hosn, Sheikh Zayed the First Street, Abu Dhabi
qasr_al_hosn.hours: Open most days; check seasonal timings. Check official site for current details
qasr_al_hosn.fee: Paid admission. Check official site for current details
qasr_al_hosn.context: Abu Dhabi's oldest stone building and the city's birthplace, originally a watchtower built in the 1790s. Now a museum tracing the emirate's history, set within a cultural site that includes the House of Artisans.
qasr_al_hosn.nudge: Combine with the adjacent House of Artisans to see traditional Emirati crafts.

heritage_village.title: Heritage Village
heritage_village.hours: Open daily; closed some afternoons. Check official site for current details
heritage_village.fee: Free admission. Check official site for current details
heritage_village.context: A reconstructed traditional Emirati village on the Breakwater depicting pre-oil Bedouin and coastal life, with a mosque, souk, and craft workshops for metalwork, pottery, and weaving.
heritage_village.nudge: Visit the workshops to watch metalwork and pottery, then enjoy the breakwater views back toward the city.

airport_transfer.title: Zayed International Airport Transfers
airport_transfer.hours: Taxis and buses operate to/from the airport daily. Check official site for current details
airport_transfer.fee: Metered taxi or low-cost express bus fare. Check official site for current details
airport_transfer.context: Zayed International Airport connects to the city via metered taxis, the A1 express bus, app-based rides, and hotel shuttles. The airport sits roughly 30 km from downtown Abu Dhabi.
airport_transfer.nudge: Pre-book an airport taxi or check the A1 express bus timings before you land.

public_transport.title: Abu Dhabi Public Transport
public_transport.hours: Buses run on published daily schedules. Check official site for current details
public_transport.fee: Low fixed fares paid via the Hafilat card. Check official site for current details
public_transport.context: Public transport is managed by the Integrated Transport Centre. City and intercity buses use the rechargeable Hafilat smart card. Taxis and e-hailing apps complement the bus network.
public_transport.nudge: Pick up a Hafilat card for buses and keep it topped up for seamless travel across the city.

visa_info.title: UAE Tourist Visa Information
visa_info.hours: Online applications available anytime. Check official site for current details
visa_info.fee: Varies by nationality and visa type; some are visa-on-arrival. Check official site for current details
visa_info.context: Many nationalities receive a visa on arrival or visa-free entry to the UAE, while others must apply in advance, typically through an airline, hotel, or the official government portal. Requirements depend on passport and trip length.
visa_info.nudge: Confirm whether your nationality qualifies for visa-on-arrival before booking your trip.

culture_etiquette.title: Culture & Etiquette in Abu Dhabi
culture_etiquette.context: Abu Dhabi is welcoming and cosmopolitan but rooted in Emirati and Islamic traditions. Visitors should dress modestly in public and religious sites, show respect during Ramadan, ask before photographing people, and avoid public displays of affection.
culture_etiquette.nudge: Dress modestly at religious and government sites and ask before photographing people.
